assumptions: The declared dividend has already been recognised as an intragroup receivable/payable, so the exposure is analysed as a recognised monetary item rather than as a forecast transaction.
The question concerns hedge accounting in consolidated financial statements under IFRS 9 for group entities exposed to foreign currency risk.

approaches.label: Hedge of intragroup monetary item
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 permits, as an exception in consolidated financial statements, the foreign currency risk of an intragroup monetary item if the related exchange differences are not fully eliminated on consolidation.

approaches.label: General intragroup exclusion
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 states that, in consolidated financial statements, hedged items generally must be with external parties, making this the default treatment unless a specific exception applies.

approaches.label: Hedge of forecast intragroup transaction
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 provides a separate exception for highly probable forecast intragroup transactions whose foreign currency risk will affect consolidated profit or loss, which defines the pre-recognition alternative.

approaches.label: Hedge of net investment
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRIC 16 sets out a distinct consolidated hedge accounting model for foreign currency risk on a net investment in a foreign operation, which is a separate boundary case from an intragroup dividend receivable.
